key_points: Industries involve the production of goods, extraction of resources, and provision of services
Industries are classified based on raw materials, size, and ownership
Industrial systems consist of inputs, processes, and outputs
Factors affecting industrial location include availability of raw materials, power supply, labor, transport, and markets
Industrial development contributes to economic growth and employment
examples: Agro-based industry: Cotton textile industry using cotton as raw material
Mineral-based industry: Iron and steel industry using iron ore and coal
Marine-based industry: Fishing industry using ocean resources
important_terms: Industrial System: A system consisting of inputs, processes, and outputs in production
Agro-based Industry: Industry using agricultural products as raw materials
Mineral-based Industry: Industry using mineral resources such as iron ore
Small-scale Industry: Industry with limited capital and workforce
Large-scale Industry: Industry with large capital investment and advanced machinery
exam_tips: Understand the classification of industries based on raw materials, size, and ownership
Remember the components of the industrial system: inputs, processes, and outputs
Study examples of agro-based, mineral-based, and marine-based industries
Learn the major factors influencing the location of industries
Understand the importance of industries in economic development
